title: F1 + F2 — Import state.json atomic write + corruption recovery + events.jsonl single-line<4KB constraint comments
description: Import SKILL.md: change .fabric/.import-state.json write to two-step (Write → .tmp file, then Bash `mv` for atomic rename); Phase 0 gains .tmp residue scan (mtime comparison; delete on parse failure or stale mtime). Add corruption-recovery chapter (JSON parse + required-field check + phase enum validation; on failure → discard and restart). All three skills: add comment near events.jsonl emit sites: '⚠️ events.jsonl is single-line JSON, target <4KB per event; long fields (session_context, source_sessions etc.) MUST self-truncate to avoid line-length overflow.'

files.target: state.json write step + Phase 0 + new corruption-recovery chapter + events.jsonl comment
files.change: Rewrite state.json persistence: 'Step A: Write content to .fabric/.import-state.json.tmp; Step B: Bash `mv .fabric/.import-state.json.tmp .fabric/.import-state.json` (atomic on POSIX).' Add Phase 0 .tmp scan: enumerate .fabric/.import-state.json.tmp; if mtime >5min stale OR fails JSON.parse → unlink. Add 'Corruption Recovery' chapter: on state.json load failure (parse error / missing required fields / phase not in enum {idle, mining, proposing, reviewing, done}) → unlink state file and restart pipeline from Phase 0. Add events.jsonl emit comment block.
Add same events.jsonl single-line<4KB constraint comment near each appendEventLedgerEvent reference; emphasize session_context + source_sessions self-truncation

implementation: In fabric-import SKILL.md find current state.json write instruction; replace with two-step pattern: 'Write .tmp first, then Bash `mv` to commit atomically (POSIX rename guarantees atomicity on same filesystem)'
Add Phase 0 .tmp residue scan: 'Before reading state.json, list .fabric/.import-state.json.tmp; if present: try JSON.parse, if fails OR mtime is >5 minutes old → unlink as crash residue'

Draft 'Corruption Recovery' chapter (placed after Phase 0): 'When state.json load fails — (a) JSON.parse throws, (b) required fields missing (phase, started_at, processed_count), (c) phase value not in {idle, mining, proposing, reviewing, done} — discard the file via unlink and restart pipeline from Phase 0 emit-side. Do NOT attempt partial recovery.'

Draft events.jsonl constraint comment block (canonical wording shared across three skills): '⚠️ events.jsonl is JSON-Lines (one event per line, append-only). Each line MUST be <4KB to avoid downstream parser line-length overflow. Long fields — session_context, source_sessions, recent_paths, user_messages_summary — MUST self-truncate (e.g. session_context first 500 chars; source_sessions cap at 5 entries). Never serialize multiline strings (escape \n).'
Insert events.jsonl comment near each appendEventLedgerEvent / emit instruction in all three templates
Mirror to .claude and .codex; run install-skills-and-hooks.test.ts and lint-protected-tokens

reference.pattern: Atomic write via .tmp + mv; corruption recovery via discard-and-restart; events.jsonl line-length constraint
reference.examples: Server-side atomicWriteText already uses temp+rename for canonical entries; this task surfaces the same pattern to client-side skill state writes

criteria: fabric-import SKILL.md state.json write uses two-step .tmp + mv pattern (greppable for `.import-state.json.tmp`)
Phase 0 .tmp residue scan documented with mtime comparison + JSON.parse check
Corruption Recovery chapter present with explicit conditions (parse error + missing fields + phase enum mismatch)
events.jsonl single-line<4KB comment present in all 3 templates near emit sites
Comment specifies which fields to self-truncate (session_context, source_sessions, recent_paths)
Three-mirror byte-identity preserved for all 9 SKILL.md files

rationale.chosen_approach: Two-step .tmp+mv atomic write + discard-and-restart on corruption + explicit line-length constraint comment
alternatives_considered: Attempt partial recovery on corruption (complex, error-prone)
decision_factors: POSIX rename atomicity is well-known + reliable
Skill state is small + cheap to regenerate
events.jsonl downstream parsers have observed line-length issues
rationale.tradeoffs: Discard-and-restart loses partial progress; mitigated because skill state is checkpoint-only and Phase 0 is cheap

manual_checks: Write a corrupted state.json (truncate mid-write); run import → verify skill discards + restarts from Phase 0
Inject a .tmp file with old mtime; run Phase 0 → verify .tmp unlinked
Read events.jsonl emit sites in all three templates → verify constraint comment present
success_metrics: Atomic write pattern present in import
